exercises.title: Group 1 Verbs — Final Kana Shifts
exercises.intro: Group 1 verbs end in an u-row kana. To make the polite ます-form, change that final kana to the matching i-row kana and add ます.
exercises.takeaway: For Group 1, shift the final u-row kana to i-row, then add ます.

exercises.prompt: Which polite form correctly comes from the Group 1 verb のむ?
Say 'I wrote a letter' using the past polite form of 'write':

slot.hint: The past polite form of the Group 1 verb meaning 'to write'.

exercises.speakText: てがみを かきました。

exercises.prompt: Invite someone for coffee using the polite negative question form:

slot.hint: The present negative polite form of the Group 1 verb meaning 'to drink'.

exercises.translation: Would you like to drink coffee?
exercises.speakText: コーヒーを のみませんか。

exercises.title: Group 2 Verbs — Drop る
exercises.intro: Group 2 verbs usually end in いる or える. Their ます-form is shorter than Group 1: drop る and add ます.
exercises.takeaway: For Group 2, drop る and add ます.

exercises.prompt: Which polite form correctly comes from the Group 2 verb たべる?
Say 'I wake up at 6 every morning' using the polite form of 'wake up':
patternJp: まいあさ ろくじに

slot.hint: The polite form of the Group 2 verb meaning 'to wake up'.

exercises.translation: I wake up at 6 every morning.
exercises.speakText: まいあさ ろくじに おきます。

exercises.prompt: Say 'I watched a movie yesterday' using the past polite form of 'watch':

slot.hint: The past polite form of the Group 2 verb meaning 'to see or watch'.

exercises.speakText: きのう えいがを みました。

exercises.title: Group 3 Verbs — Irregular
exercises.intro: Group 3 has only the irregular verbs する and くる. Many noun + する verbs work by changing only the する part.
rows: べんきょうします / benkyou shimasu
うんどうします / undou shimasu
りょこうします / ryokou shimasu
そうじします / souji shimasu
exercises.takeaway: Memorise する → します and くる → きます; noun + する keeps the noun unchanged.

exercises.prompt: Which polite form correctly comes from the irregular verb くる?
Say 'I study Japanese' using the する compound verb:
patternJp: にほんごを べんきょう

slot.hint: The polite form of the irregular verb meaning 'to do'.

exercises.speakText: にほんごを べんきょうします。

exercises.prompt: Say 'A friend is coming' using the irregular polite form:

slot.hint: The polite form of the irregular verb meaning 'to come'.

exercises.speakText: ともだちが きます。

exercises.title: ます-Form Conjugation
exercises.intro: Once you have the ます-form, polite present, negative, past, and past negative endings follow a regular pattern. The same endings attach after the ます-stem.
rows: たべませんでした / tabemasen deshita
かきませんでした / kakimasen deshita
exercises.takeaway: After the ます-stem, use ます, ません, ました, or ませんでした.

exercises.prompt: Which ending means polite past negative?
Say 'I did not go to school yesterday' using polite past negative:
patternJp: きのう がっこうに

slot.hint: The past negative polite form of the verb meaning 'to go'.

exercises.translation: I didn't go to school yesterday.
exercises.speakText: きのう がっこうに いきませんでした。

exercises.prompt: Say 'Let's eat together' using the polite suggestion form:

slot.hint: The polite suggestion form of the Group 2 verb meaning 'to eat'.

exercises.speakText: いっしょに たべましょう。

exercises.prompt: Which conversion correctly uses the Group 2 shortcut?
exercises.title: Group 2 shortcut

variants.label: CORRECT
variants.note: The vowel before る is e, so this is Group 2: drop る and add ます.

variants.label: COMMON MISTAKE
variants.jp: たべる → たべります
variants.note: Group 2 does not shift to り; it simply drops る.

variants.label: DIFFERENT MEANING
variants.jp: かえる → かえります
variants.note: This is correct Japanese, but かえる is a tricky Group 1 verb, not the Group 2 shortcut.

variants.label: WRONG
variants.note: かえる meaning 'to return' is Group 1, so it becomes かえります.

exercises.takeaway: Group 2 usually drops る, but memorise the tricky Group 1 verbs that look similar.
exercises.speakText: たべる、たべます。

exercises.prompt: Which conversion correctly applies the Group 1 ます rule?
exercises.title: Group 1 ます rule

variants.label: CORRECT
variants.note: む shifts to み, then ます is added.

variants.label: COMMON MISTAKE
variants.note: Never attach ます directly to a Group 1 dictionary form.

variants.label: WRONG
variants.note: The ます-form uses the i-row, not the e-row.

variants.label: DIFFERENT MEANING
variants.note: This is also Group 1, but it is not the verb in the prompt.

exercises.takeaway: For Group 1, change only the final kana to its i-row equivalent before adding ます.
exercises.speakText: のむ、のみます。

exercises.prompt: Which sentence is a polite invitation to eat together?
exercises.title: ～ませんか — polite invitation

variants.label: CORRECT
variants.jp: いっしょに たべませんか。
variants.note: ～ませんか makes a soft, polite invitation.

variants.label: DIFFERENT MEANING
variants.jp: コーヒーを のみませんか。
variants.en: Would you like to drink coffee?
variants.note: This is the same invitation pattern, but it invites someone for coffee, not eating together.

variants.label: DIFFERENT MEANING
variants.jp: いっしょに たべましょう。
variants.note: ～ましょう is a direct suggestion, while the prompt asks for the softer ～ませんか invitation.

variants.label: WRONG
variants.jp: いっしょに たべますか。
variants.note: This is a question with ますか, not the polite invitation pattern.

exercises.takeaway: Use ～ませんか for a soft invitation; ～ましょう is a stronger 'let's'.
exercises.speakText: いっしょに たべませんか。

exercises.prompt: Which sentence correctly uses an irregular する compound?
exercises.title: Irregular verbs: する→します, くる→きます

variants.label: CORRECT
variants.note: Noun + する becomes a verb, and する changes to します.

variants.label: WRONG
variants.note: Do not attach ます directly to する; use します.

variants.label: DIFFERENT MEANING
variants.note: This is the correct polite form of くる, but it is not a する compound.

variants.label: DIFFERENT MEANING
variants.note: きります comes from きる 'to cut', not くる 'to come'.

exercises.takeaway: する becomes します in compounds; くる becomes きます, which is different from きります.
exercises.speakText: べんきょうします。

exercises.title: Verb Groups & ます-Form in Context
exercises.intro: These are the core sentence patterns from the lesson in context. Listen for the verb ending and connect it back to the verb group.
rows: まいあさ ろくじに おきます。
I wake up at 6 every morning.
にほんごを べんきょうします。
コーヒーを のみませんか。
Would you like to drink coffee?
しゅうまつ なにを しますか。
What will you do on the weekend?
きのう えいがを みました。
きのう がっこうに いきませんでした。
I didn't go to school yesterday.
てがみを かきました。
ともだちが きます。
exercises.takeaway: Identify the verb group first, then read the ます ending for tense and polarity.

exercises.prompt: Listen and choose the correct English meaning:
exercises.speakText: まいあさ ろくじに おきます。
options: I wake up at 6 every morning.
I left at 6 in the morning.

exercises.prompt: Listen and choose the correct English meaning:
exercises.speakText: にほんごを べんきょうします。

exercises.prompt: Listen and choose the correct English meaning:
exercises.speakText: きのう えいがを みました。
options: I want to watch a movie.
I will watch a movie tomorrow.

exercises.prompt: Listen and choose the correct English meaning:
exercises.speakText: コーヒーを のみませんか。
options: Would you like to drink coffee?

exercises.prompt: Listen and choose the correct English meaning:
exercises.speakText: ともだちが きます。
options: I am going to a friend's place.

exercises.prompt: Listen and choose the correct English meaning:
exercises.speakText: てがみを かきました。

exercises.prompt: Listen and choose the correct English meaning:
exercises.speakText: しゅうまつ なにを しますか。
options: What did you do on the weekend?
What will you do on the weekend?
Where are you going this weekend?

exercises.prompt: Listen and choose the correct English meaning:
exercises.speakText: きのう がっこうに いきませんでした。
options: I go to school every day.
I didn't go to school yesterday.
I went to school this morning.

exercises.prompt: Listen and type what you hear in romaji:
exercises.speakText: おきます

exercises.prompt: Listen and type what you hear in romaji:
exercises.speakText: たべました

exercises.prompt: Listen and type what you hear in romaji:
exercises.speakText: のみません

exercises.prompt: Listen and type what you hear in romaji:
exercises.speakText: します

exercises.prompt: Listen and type what you hear in romaji:
exercises.speakText: きます

exercises.prompt: Listen and type what you hear in romaji:
exercises.speakText: かきました

exercises.prompt: Listen and type what you hear in romaji:
exercises.speakText: いきませんでした

exercises.prompt: Match each verb to its meaning:

exercises.title: Reading practice — verb groups and ます-form

cards.jp: まいあさ ろくじに おきます。
cards.en: I wake up at 6 every morning. (Group 2)

cards.jp: にほんごを べんきょうします。
cards.en: I study Japanese. (する compound verb)

cards.jp: きのう えいがを みました。
cards.en: I watched a movie yesterday. (Group 2 past)

cards.jp: コーヒーを のみませんか。
cards.en: Would you like to drink coffee? (Group 1 invitation)

cards.jp: きのう がっこうに いきませんでした。
cards.en: I didn't go to school yesterday. (Group 1 past negative)

cards.jp: しゅうまつ なにを しますか。
cards.en: What will you do on the weekend? (irregular)

cards.jp: ともだちが きます。
てがみを かきました。
いっしょに たべましょう。
まいにち うんどうします。
cards.en: I exercise every day. (する compound)
